description:
A utility-scale CSP developer has received an $80/MWh power purchase agreement (PPA) offer for a 50 MW parabolic trough plant near Daggett, California (lat=34.867, lon=-116.783) — one of the highest direct normal irradiance (DNI) sites in the United States. The plant design includes 6 hours of thermal energy storage (TES). The development team needs to determine the optimal solar multiple (SM) before committing to a collector field size.

Plant design parameters:
- Nameplate capacity: 50 MW (net)
- Thermal energy storage: 6 hours (fixed for this study)
- Location: Daggett, CA — use the bundled Daggett weather file in SAM's solar resource library
- Capital cost: $7,500/kW total installed, O&M: $65/kW-yr
- Project life: 30 years, discount rate: 8.5%, federal ITC: 30%
- PPA price: $0.080/kWh, escalation: 0%/yr

Conduct a parametric study of solar multiples from 1.0 to 3.0 in increments of 0.25 (9 values total). For each solar multiple, record the annual energy output (MWh/yr), capacity factor (%), real LCOE ($/MWh), and NPV ($). Identify which solar multiple maximizes project NPV.

Save results to: /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/Daggett_CSP_SM_Analysis.json

The JSON must contain a 'configurations' array (9 entries, each with: solar_multiple, annual_energy_mwh, capacity_factor_pct, lcoe_real_usd_per_mwh, npv_usd), an 'optimal_solar_multiple' field, a 'site' field ('Daggett, CA'), and an 'analysis_summary' field.
